instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
How Is Technology Impacting the Healthcare Industry?
The advancement in the healthcare industry has been in leaps and bounds. The effect of these technological developments can be easily seen as countless patients are being saved, and the quality of life is being constantly improved. The importance of technology in health has indeed been massive, and with the advent of cutting-edge tools and techniques, patients are able to receive treatments that are minimally invasive, bloodless, and far less painful.
The onset of the pandemic prompted the need for improved healthcare facilities. Listed below are some of the significant advancements made in the healthcare industry:
The use of electronic health records
Nothing less than an evolution of electronic health records, also called EHRs, has been a major transformation in how technology impacts the healthcare industry. All major hospitals today are better able to store, use and share patient records. The introduction of this standardised system for creating and maintaining records by healthcare providers has been clinically relevant. With the use of EHR, a person can visit any health service provider and yet have the access to his/ her health records in an electronic format. EHR acts as a living document and thus empowers the patient.
The use of telemedicineOver the past few years, the need for an e-health system has been increasing, and covid-19 pushed us to make the switch quickly. As the name suggests, telemedicine is the diagnosis and treatment based on telecommunication. Telemedicine modernises the patient’s experiences as healthcare services can be accessed remotely.
Online consultation, where a patient sitting far away can connect to his doctor via a video call, is just an example of the various advantages of telemedicine. Telemedicine has not only made things efficient and convenient for the consumer but is also helping healthcare practitioners collaborate from different cities/ towns and even countries.
Remote monitoring tools Thanks to the availability of remote monitoring tools, millions of patients were able to monitor their health at home. It is not just cost-cutting, but the time and effort that these monitoring tools can save are remarkable. A doctor, even when outside the clinical setting, can get a real-time understanding of the patient’s condition, further helping them to make proactive decisions. Patients also get the freedom to engage as well as understand the pattern in their everyday health conditions. Pulse oximeters, glucometers, blood pressure cuffs etc, have greatly stressed the importance of technology in healthcare.
Wearable technologyAnother area that shows how technology has impacted healthcare is the introduction of wearable technology, and there has been a jump in their demand. In healthcare, wearable technology refers to the electronic devices that people wear, for example, smart watches or Fitbit watches, biosensors, wearable BP monitors, wearable ECG monitors etc., that are designed to gather and compile information about an individual’s level of activity and other aspects and thus their health. One can also use the device to share their health data directly with a doctor or trainer, or healthcare professional.
Genome sequencingGenomics has been another game-changer, let us see how it can bring together health and technology. Genomics deals with the study of the genes present in an organism. By deciphering DNA sequencing and genomes, better insights into a person’s health conditions can be made. This can further help the person, and their caretakers/ doctors make well-informed medical decisions. Genome sequencing has allowed remarkable improvements in the diagnosis, treatment as well as prevention of diseases.
RoboticsRobotics is probably one of the fastest-developing fields of medicine. The use of robots in healthcare continues to improve surgical procedures, patient care, and operational efficiencies. Not just in the clinical department, but robots are now being used for a much wider range of duties, especially post covid-19 to reduce the exposure of pathogens.
Conclusion
While there is no denying that India has come far in the field of medicine and healthcare facilities, there is still a long way to go. We live in a world of innovations and development, and healthcare is expected to revolutionise further in the coming years. India needs to harness technology in a more efficient way. The industry has to realise the importance of technology in health and make the enormous yet untapped potential. All the players of the ecosystem, like healthcare providers, life science experts, medical analysts, equipment manufacturers, and suppliers, have to hand in hand.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article is not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
